text: The door is closed.
rationale: Antonym of 'open', but an AFFIRMATIVE assertion. A valence-confounded axis scores this as 'no'.

text: The door is not closed.
rationale: Negation of an antonym. Truth-conditionally ≈ HC-open, but the polarity marker is present.

text: The task is impossible.
rationale: Affixal antonym. Affirmative assertion carrying a negative morpheme.

text: The task is not possible.
rationale: Syntactic negation. Near-synonymous with HC03 but structurally opposite.

text: It is not impossible.
rationale: Two negations, net affirmative — and weaker than plain 'possible'.

text: She is not unhappy.
rationale: Litotes. Net positive, hedged. Cosine-similar to 'She is unhappy'.

text: I never said I didn't do it.
rationale: Stacked negation with scope interaction.

text: No vi nada.
rationale: Two negative words, one negation. Token-counting probes read this as double.

text: Я никогда никому ничего не говорил.
rationale: Four negative words, one negation.

text: אף אחד לא הגיע.
rationale: Obligatory concord: af echad + lo.

text: Few people attended.
rationale: Downward-entailing, licenses NPIs, but NOT negation.

text: She rarely eats meat.
rationale: Adverbial quantifier, not sentential negation.

text: He left without saying goodbye.
rationale: 'without' is inherently negative but the clause is asserted.

text: I doubt he will come.
rationale: Lexical negativity inside an affirmative matrix.

text: He failed to submit the form.
rationale: Implicative verb: entails he did not submit. No negative morpheme at all.

text: All that glitters is not gold.
rationale: ¬∀ vs ∀¬. Genuinely ambiguous; DO NOT use as a labelled example.

text: I didn't come because I was tired.
rationale: Negation can scope over the verb or over the because-clause.

text: I don't think he will come.
rationale: Neg-raising: surface matrix negation, semantic complement negation.

text: I think he will not come.
rationale: Same meaning as HC18, different structure. Should sit at the same place on the axis.

text: He said the door is not open.
rationale: The speaker asserts a report. The negation belongs to the reported clause.

text: It is false that the door is not open.
rationale: Negation under a falsity operator.

text: She denied that she had signed the contract.
rationale: Negation carried by the matrix verb, not by a particle.

text: The report does not say the permit was denied.
rationale: Two negations at different levels. Metalinguistic scope.

text: Isn't the door open?
rationale: Negative polar question: biased toward the POSITIVE. Not an assertion.

text: Is the door not open?
rationale: Same surface negation, different bias. Never label as 'no'.

text: The door is open, isn't it?
rationale: Affirmative assertion with negative tag.

text: He didn't walk — he ran.
rationale: Metalinguistic negation: the event happened, the description is corrected.

text: I didn't buy the RED car.
rationale: Focus-sensitive negation; scope depends on prosody, invisible in text.

text: I don't have any money.
rationale: 'any' licensed by negation.

text: If you have any questions, ask.
rationale: 'any' licensed by a conditional, not negation.

text: Q: You don't smoke? A: No. (= I don't smoke)
rationale: English NO agrees with the negative question.

text: Q: たばこを吸わないんですか。 A: はい。(= 吸いません)
rationale: Japanese HAI on a negative question means 'correct, I don't'. Surface 'yes', semantic 'no'.

text: Q: 담배 안 피우세요? A: 네. (= 안 피웁니다)
rationale: Same inversion as Japanese.

text: Q: Tu ne fumes pas ? A: Si. (= je fume)
rationale: French SI: a 'yes' word that only exists to contradict a negative.

text: Q: Du rauchst nicht? A: Doch. (= ich rauche)
rationale: German DOCH, same function.

text: Q: ألا تدخن؟ A: بلى. (= أدخن)
rationale: Arabic BALĀ contradicts the negative; NAʿAM would confirm it.

text: Q: An bhfuil an doras oscailte? A: Níl.
rationale: Irish has no 'no'. The negative answer IS the negated verb.

text: Q: 你去不去? A: 不去。
rationale: Mandarin has no general 'no'. The answer negates the echoed verb.

text: Q: Tuletko? A: En.
rationale: Finnish 'en' is a first-person-singular NEGATIVE VERB, not a particle.

text: Dia belum makan.
rationale: 'Not yet' — presupposes the event is still expected. ≠ 'tidak makan'.

text: Anh ấy chưa ăn.
rationale: chưa ≠ không. Different lexeme, different presupposition.

text: მე ვერ ვცურავ.
rationale: Georgian VER: negation of ability, distinct from plain AR.

text: 나는 수영을 못 한다.
rationale: Korean 못: inability, distinct from 안.

text: The door, which had been painted last spring by the previous tenant, is open.
rationale: LONG affirmative. Guards against a probe that is really measuring length.

text: Not open.
rationale: SHORT negative. Same guard, opposite direction.

text: I do not like this restaurant.
rationale: Negative polarity AND negative sentiment. Aligned.

text: I do not dislike this restaurant.
rationale: Negative polarity, POSITIVE-ish sentiment. Anti-aligned — the key control.

text: This restaurant is terrible.
rationale: Affirmative polarity, negative sentiment. Anti-aligned.

text: Who doesn't love a good story?
rationale: Rhetorical negative question = strong affirmative.

text: J'ai peur qu'il ne vienne.
rationale: Expletive 'ne': a negative morpheme with NO negative meaning.
